---
title: "Cross-Validation: DB-Path vs Legacy-Path Assessment"
author: "nemascan-sims-nf"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
params:
  existing_assessment: ""
  db_assessment: ""
  db_dir: ""
execute:
  eval: true
  warning: true
  message: true
---

## Summary

This report compares the **DB-path** QTL assessment against the **legacy R file-based** assessment
to verify that both pipelines produce identical analytical results. The DB-path queries a
Parquet/DuckDB database populated during the pipeline run, while the legacy path uses R scripts
operating on intermediate files. Both paths analyze the same GWA output and should produce
matching QTL detection, interval boundaries, and significance calls.

The report runs 5 validation checks, then compares results at the **per-replicate** level —
each unique `(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id)` combination — across all mapping methods
and thresholds, using the `designate_qtl()` / `count_outcomes()` / `calculate_simrep_performance()`
pipeline to compute Power and FDR.

:::{.callout-note}
## Known Design Differences

| Aspect | Legacy path | DB path |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `var.exp` / `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` | Computed from genotype matrix | `NA` (no genotype matrix in DB) |
| `algorithm_id` format | `inbred_nopca_EIGEN` | `LMM-EXACT-INBRED_noPCA_EIGEN` |

Both encode the same information; the `normalize_algorithm_id()` helper strips the prefix and
uppercases for joining.
:::

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# Source the shared R library (includes sim_performance.R)
Sys.setenv(NEMASCAN_R_LIB = here::here("R"))
source(here::here("R", "setup.R"))
```

```{r}
#| label: paths
# For interactive use, set full paths here:
# existing_assessment_path <- "/full/path/to/simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_assessment_path       <- "/full/path/to/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_dir                   <- "/full/path/to/db"

existing_assessment_path <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
db_assessment_path       <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
db_dir                   <- "/Users/ryanmckeown/Desktop/nemascan-sims-nf/Analysis_Results-20260213/db"

# Read from Quarto params, fall back to env vars, then interactive vars above
if (!exists("existing_assessment_path") || existing_assessment_path == "") {
  existing_assessment_path <- if (params$existing_assessment != "") {
    params$existing_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_EXISTING_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_assessment_path") || db_assessment_path == "") {
  db_assessment_path <- if (params$db_assessment != "") {
    params$db_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_dir") || db_dir == "") {
  db_dir <- if (params$db_dir != "") {
    params$db_dir
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_DIR", unset = "")
  }
}

# Validate
stopifnot(
  "existing_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    existing_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(existing_assessment_path),
  "db_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    db_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(db_assessment_path),
  "db_dir not set or directory missing" =
    db_dir != "" && dir.exists(db_dir)
)
message("Existing assessment: ", existing_assessment_path)
message("DB assessment:       ", db_assessment_path)
message("DB directory:        ", db_dir)
```

```{r}
#| label: theme-and-helpers

# ggplot2 theme for cross-validation plots
theme_xval <- ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
ggplot2::theme_set(theme_xval)

path_colors <- c("Legacy" = "#E69F00", "DB" = "#56B4E9")
designation_colors <- c(
  "Detected.CV" = "#2ca02c",
  "Missed.CV" = "#d62728",
  "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval" = "#ff7f0e",
  "False.Discovery" = "#9467bd"
)

# Parse legacy assessment TSV (no header, 35-column schema from Assess_Sims.R)
read_legacy_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS",
                  "detected.peak", "BETA", "interval.log10p",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size",
                  "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "top.hit", "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf",
                  "effect_distribution", "strain_set_id",
                  "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Parse DB assessment TSV (no header, 35-column standardized schema)
read_db_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "BETA", "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS", "detected.peak",
                  "interval.log10p", "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size", "top.hit",
                  "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "effect_distribution",
                  "strain_set_id", "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Normalize algorithm_id across both paths for joining
normalize_algorithm_id <- function(alg_id) {
  toupper(sub("^LMM-EXACT-", "", toupper(alg_id)))
}

# Create a composite key for joining rows across both assessments
make_join_key <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id),
      join_key = paste(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, effect_distribution,
                       strain_set_id, norm_alg, QTL, sep = "|")
    )
}

# Create method label from mode/type/threshold for stratified analysis
parse_method_label <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id),
      method_label = paste(
        gsub("_", "/", sub("_[A-Z]+$", "", norm_alg)),  # mode/pca
        sub(".*_", "", norm_alg),                         # threshold
        sep = " "
      )
    )
}

# HTML badge for validation summary
make_badge <- function(passed, label = NULL) {
  color <- if (passed) "#28a745" else "#dc3545"
  text <- if (is.null(label)) (if (passed) "PASS" else "FAIL") else label
  sprintf('<span style="background:%s;color:white;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:4px;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.85em;">%s</span>',
          color, text)
}
```

## Data Loading & Overview

```{r}
#| label: load-data
existing <- read_legacy_assessment(existing_assessment_path)
db_assess <- read_db_assessment(db_assessment_path)

message("Existing assessment: ", nrow(existing), " rows x ", ncol(existing), " cols")
message("DB assessment:       ", nrow(db_assess), " rows x ", ncol(db_assess), " cols")
```

```{r}
#| label: designate-and-label
# Apply designate_qtl() from sim_performance.R to classify outcomes
existing_d <- existing %>%
  designate_qtl() %>%
  parse_method_label() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(path = "Legacy")
db_d <- db_assess %>%
  designate_qtl() %>%
  parse_method_label() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(path = "DB")
```

```{r}
#| label: data-dimensions
#| results: asis
dim_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Rows", "Unique methods", "Threshold types",
             "Detected.CV", "Missed.CV", "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", "False.Discovery"),
  Legacy = c(
    nrow(existing_d),
    length(unique(existing_d$method_label)),
    paste(sort(unique(existing_d$threshold)), collapse = ", "),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "Detected.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "Missed.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "False.Discovery", na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  DB = c(
    nrow(db_d),
    length(unique(db_d$method_label)),
    paste(sort(unique(db_d$threshold)), collapse = ", "),
    sum(db_d$designation == "Detected.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "Missed.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "False.Discovery", na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)
knitr::kable(dim_table, caption = "Data Dimensions & Designation Counts")
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Legacy Data

```{r}
#| label: preview-legacy
knitr::kable(head(existing, 5))
```

### DB Data

```{r}
#| label: preview-db
knitr::kable(head(db_assess, 5))
```

:::

## Per-Replicate Concordance

This section computes three concordance scores for each **mapping entry** — a unique combination
of simulation replicate `(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id)`, mapping method, and
significance threshold. Each score ranges from 0 (no concordance) to 1 (perfect concordance)
between the DB and legacy assessment paths.

| Score | What it measures | 1 means | 0 means |
|-------|-----------------|---------|---------|
| Detection | Detected/Simulated status agreement per QTL | All QTL rows agree on Detected flag | No QTL rows agree |
| Designation | `designate_qtl()` category agreement per QTL | All designations match | No designations match |
| Interval Overlap | Geometric overlap of QTL intervals (Jaccard) | Start and end positions match perfectly | No overlap between intervals |

```{r}
#| label: replicate-join
# Join the two paths on the full composite key (includes QTL identity)
existing_keyed <- existing_d %>% make_join_key()
db_keyed <- db_d %>% make_join_key()

joined <- dplyr::inner_join(
  existing_keyed %>%
    dplyr::select(join_key, method_label, norm_alg, threshold,
                  nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id,
                  designation_legacy = designation,
                  Detected_legacy = Detected, Simulated_legacy = Simulated,
                  log10p_legacy = log10p,
                  startPOS_legacy = startPOS, peakPOS_legacy = peakPOS,
                  endPOS_legacy = endPOS),
  db_keyed %>%
    dplyr::select(join_key,
                  designation_db = designation,
                  Detected_db = Detected, Simulated_db = Simulated,
                  log10p_db = log10p,
                  startPOS_db = startPOS, peakPOS_db = peakPOS,
                  endPOS_db = endPOS),
  by = "join_key"
)

message("Joined rows: ", nrow(joined))
```

```{r}
#| label: concordance-helpers

# Replicate grouping: replicate identity + method + threshold
rep_group <- c("nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "strain_set_id")
mapping_group <- c(rep_group, "method_label", "threshold")

# Interval overlap (Jaccard): intersection / union of [start, end] ranges.
# Returns 1 when start and end match perfectly, 0 when no overlap.
interval_jaccard <- function(start_a, end_a, start_b, end_b) {
  intersection <- pmax(0, pmin(end_a, end_b) - pmax(start_a, start_b))
  union <- pmax(end_a, end_b) - pmin(start_a, start_b)
  ifelse(union == 0, 1, intersection / union)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: compute-concordance-scores

# --- Score 1: Detection Concordance ---
# Fraction of QTL rows where Detected flag agrees
detection_conc <- joined %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group))) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_qtl = dplyr::n(),
    n_detect_agree = sum(Detected_legacy == Detected_db),
    detection_concordance = n_detect_agree / n_qtl,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Score 2: Designation Concordance ---
# Fraction of QTL rows where designation category agrees
designation_conc <- joined %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(designation_legacy), !is.na(designation_db)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group))) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_designated = dplyr::n(),
    n_desig_agree = sum(designation_legacy == designation_db),
    designation_concordance = n_desig_agree / n_designated,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Score 3: Interval Overlap Concordance ---
# For QTLs detected by both paths, Jaccard overlap of [startPOS, endPOS].
# Per-QTL scores are averaged to a per-mapping score.
interval_conc_per_qtl <- joined %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    Detected_legacy == "TRUE", Detected_db == "TRUE",
    !is.na(startPOS_legacy), !is.na(startPOS_db),
    !is.na(endPOS_legacy), !is.na(endPOS_db)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    qtl_interval_jaccard = interval_jaccard(
      startPOS_legacy, endPOS_legacy, startPOS_db, endPOS_db
    )
  )

interval_conc <- interval_conc_per_qtl %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group))) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_intervals = dplyr::n(),
    n_perfect = sum(qtl_interval_jaccard == 1),
    interval_concordance = mean(qtl_interval_jaccard),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Combine all three scores into one table ---
concordance <- detection_conc %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group), n_qtl, detection_concordance) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    designation_conc %>%
      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group), designation_concordance),
    by = mapping_group
  ) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    interval_conc %>%
      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group), n_intervals, n_perfect,
                    interval_concordance),
    by = mapping_group
  )

message("Mapping entries scored: ", nrow(concordance))
```

### Per-Mapping Concordance Scores

Each row is one mapping entry (replicate + method + threshold). The three scores quantify how
well the DB path reproduces the legacy path for that specific mapping.

```{r}
#| label: concordance-table
#| results: asis
display_cols <- c(mapping_group, "n_qtl",
                  "detection_concordance", "designation_concordance",
                  "n_intervals", "interval_concordance")
concordance_display <- concordance %>%
  dplyr::select(dplyr::any_of(display_cols)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    detection_concordance = sprintf("%.3f", detection_concordance),
    designation_concordance = ifelse(is.na(designation_concordance), "-",
                                     sprintf("%.3f", designation_concordance)),
    interval_concordance = ifelse(is.na(interval_concordance), "-",
                                   sprintf("%.3f", interval_concordance)),
    n_intervals = ifelse(is.na(n_intervals), 0, n_intervals)
  )

knitr::kable(concordance_display, caption = "Per-Mapping Concordance Scores")
```

### Score Distributions

```{r}
#| label: viz-concordance-distributions
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of concordance scores across all mapping entries"
#| fig-height: 4

score_long <- concordance %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(detection_concordance, designation_concordance, interval_concordance),
    names_to = "score_type",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(score)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(score_type = gsub("_concordance", "", score_type) %>%
                  gsub("^(.)", "\\U\\1", ., perl = TRUE))

ggplot2::ggplot(score_long, ggplot2::aes(x = score, fill = score_type)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, boundary = 0, color = "white") +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ score_type, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)) +
  ggplot2::labs(x = "Concordance Score", y = "Count", fill = "Score") +
  ggplot2::guides(fill = "none")
```

### Average Concordance by Method and Threshold

Concordance scores averaged across all replicates within each method and threshold combination.

```{r}
#| label: avg-concordance-by-method
#| results: asis
avg_concordance <- concordance %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method_label, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_replicates = dplyr::n(),
    mean_detection = mean(detection_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_designation = mean(designation_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_interval = mean(interval_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean_detection = sprintf("%.4f", mean_detection),
    mean_designation = ifelse(is.nan(as.numeric(mean_designation)), "-",
                               sprintf("%.4f", as.numeric(mean_designation))),
    mean_interval = ifelse(is.nan(as.numeric(mean_interval)), "-",
                            sprintf("%.4f", as.numeric(mean_interval)))
  )

knitr::kable(avg_concordance,
             col.names = c("Method", "Threshold", "N Replicates",
                           "Detection", "Designation", "Interval"),
             caption = "Mean Concordance by Method and Threshold")
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-avg-concordance-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Mean concordance scores by method and threshold"
#| fig-height: 5

avg_long <- concordance %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method_label, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Detection = mean(detection_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    Designation = mean(designation_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    Interval = mean(interval_concordance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Detection, Designation, Interval),
    names_to = "score_type",
    values_to = "mean_score"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.nan(mean_score))

ggplot2::ggplot(avg_long,
                ggplot2::aes(x = score_type,
                             y = paste(method_label, threshold, sep = " / "),
                             fill = mean_score)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_score)), size = 4) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fee0d2", high = "#2ca02c", limits = c(0, 1)) +
  ggplot2::labs(x = "Concordance Score", y = "Method / Threshold", fill = "Mean Score")
```

### Per-QTL Interval Concordance Detail

For each QTL interval detected by both paths, this shows the individual Jaccard overlap score.
A score of 1.0 means start and end positions match perfectly; lower values indicate partial
overlap.

```{r}
#| label: viz-interval-jaccard-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Per-QTL interval Jaccard scores, faceted by method"
#| eval: !expr nrow(interval_conc_per_qtl) > 0

ggplot2::ggplot(interval_conc_per_qtl,
                ggplot2::aes(x = reorder(join_key, qtl_interval_jaccard),
                             y = qtl_interval_jaccard,
                             color = threshold)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ method_label, scales = "free_x") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.05)) +
  ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, y = "Interval Jaccard Score", color = "Threshold") +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
                 axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: interval-concordance-summary
#| results: asis

if (nrow(interval_conc_per_qtl) > 0) {
  n_perfect <- sum(interval_conc_per_qtl$qtl_interval_jaccard == 1)
  n_total <- nrow(interval_conc_per_qtl)
  n_partial <- n_total - n_perfect

  if (n_partial == 0) {
    cat(make_badge(TRUE),
        sprintf(" All %d detected QTL intervals match perfectly (Jaccard = 1.0).\n",
                n_total))
  } else {
    cat(make_badge(FALSE),
        sprintf(" %d of %d intervals have imperfect overlap.\n\n",
                n_partial, n_total))
    imperfect <- interval_conc_per_qtl %>%
      dplyr::filter(qtl_interval_jaccard < 1) %>%
      dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(mapping_group), join_key, qtl_interval_jaccard,
                    startPOS_legacy, endPOS_legacy, startPOS_db, endPOS_db) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(qtl_interval_jaccard = sprintf("%.4f", qtl_interval_jaccard))
    print(knitr::kable(imperfect, caption = "QTL Intervals with Imperfect Overlap"))
  }
} else {
  cat("No QTL intervals detected by both paths to compare.\n")
}
```
